prevalence.source: European adolescent health surveys and gynecology studies
prevalence.trend: stable; improved recognition of secondary causes

incidence.value: Common at menarche; recurrent monthly episodes

vulnerablePopulations: women with limited access to specialist care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis for primary dysmenorrhea; investigate secondary causes when severe or refractory

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions of women affected
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with NSAIDs and hormonal therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and gynecology assessment; pelvic ultrasound for secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: NSAIDs, combined oral contraceptives/progestins, non-pharmacologic measures; referral for suspected endometriosis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delays in diagnosing secondary causes in some systems
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad access to effective medications and gynecology services
